title: pilosella echioides subsp. procera
intro: Pilosella echioides subsp. procera, also known as tall hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. Pilosella echioides subsp. procera can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
